title: We’ve Made a Mess of Masculinity, but I Believe It Can Change
description:
My partner and I go to the movies. The theater and our conversations after, on the drive home or at the bar where we’re regulars—this routine is our most consistent intimacy. We are in couples therapy, have been for many years now. The current cadence is once a month. In this season of my 30s, […]

The post We’ve Made a Mess of Masculinity, but I Believe It Can Change appeared
hook: He watches movies with his partner every week—their only consistent intimacy while navigating couples therapy and the wreckage of modern manhood.

title: A new Mary Oliver documentary captures the poet’s wild and precious life.
description: At the time of her death in 2019, the late Mary Oliver was one of the most successful American poets to ever publish. She wrote dozens of collections, and several highly quotable bangers that a reader would be as likely
hook: Mary Oliver became one of America's most quotable poets by accident, and a new documentary reveals the wild life behind those bangers.

title: Book Review: ‘Fixer Chao,’ by Han Ong
description: Han Ong’s barbed, irreverent portrait of identity, class envy and flimflam deception in turn-of-the-21st-century Manhattan still resonates.
hook: Han Ong's debut novel cuts through turn-of-the-century Manhattan with the precision of someone who understands exactly how class envy works.

title: Summertime Solitude: The Green Ray at 40
description: Transforming the idyll of summer into a landscape of alienation, Éric Rohmer's piercing portrait of loneliness endures as a deeply feminine exploration of solo travel.
hook: Rohmer's 40-year-old film transforms a summer romance into a study of female loneliness so specific it still feels like a secret.

title: Jenni Fagan: ‘Maya Angelou taught me that I owed myself hope’
description:
The Scottish author on loving The Hobbit, fairytales, Frankenstein and the shock of A Clockwork Orange

My earliest reading memory
Fairytales. I was obsessed. I took fairytales very seriously as moral lessons. I soon knew that I’d always help any old lady cross the road, it really is always best to do so.

My favourite book growing up
The Hobbit was my favourite book while growing up. It expanded my unders
hook: Jenni Fagan on why she took fairytales as moral lessons and what A Clockwork Orange taught her about darkness.

title: Madonna Is Finally Giving the World What It Wants
description: The singer is known for pushing pop forward, but her most acclaimed album in years is playing it safe.
hook: Madonna's most acclaimed work in years plays it safe—and the culture finally caught up to reward her for restraint instead of provocation.

title: No Return
description: "Did an experienced alpinist leave his girlfriend to die at the top of a mountain?"
hook: A mountaineer vanishes at the summit. His girlfriend dies there. Was it accident, murder, or the kind of choice no one wants to name?
